clues: Due to significant discomfort with this condition, some desperate patients attempted to cure their disease in the late 1800s by climbing into a hole carved into the carcass of a whale and remaining inside for up to 30 hours. Patients hoped that the combination of heat, animal fats, and decaying organic matter would draw out the pain

World-renowned artists like Pierre-Auguste Renoir suffered from this disease. As it progressed, he became wheelchair-dependent with permanent deformities but he kept painting by having brushes tied directly to his hands
Characteristic joint changes might include ulnar deviation or the piano key sign. Some patients also develop firm nodules over pressure points such as the extensor surface of the forearms and elbows.

While almost all mammals can be affected by a version of this, a massive unchecked global epidemic broke out in dogs in 1978 where dogs developed sudden collapse, severe vomiting, and hemorrhagic enteritis
Before we knew what caused these illnesses, 19th and 20th century pediatricians categorized common childhood rashes into numbered lists. This was #5
While children get a bright facial rash, infected adults usually develop polyarthropathy. For those with an underlying blood disorder such as sickle cell anemia, it can cause a dangerous anemia

Symptoms of this disease were described in ancient Indian medical practice as early as 600BC where it was called Kampavata. However, the disease is named after an English Surgeon who wrote an essay about it in 1817 where he described three of his own patients and three people who he saw on the street with this disease

Patients with this disease often develop a distinctive musky odor, which was first described by a Scottish nurse with hyperosmia who noticed her husband devloped this smell years before diagnosis and later recognized the smell on other patients with this disease.
Interestingly, up to 90% of patients experience a decreased sense of smell, which often precedes classic motor symptoms such as bradykinesia, rigidity, and a 4-6Hz tremor
Originally known as LCZ696, this medication is the first drug-drug co-crystal where two medications are combined into a single, highly specialized crystal lattice structure

Studied in the landmark trial PARADIGM-HF for adults, it was also approved to treat children 1 year and older after the PANORAMA study. For young patients or adults who struggle with pills, it is available in sprinkle oral pellets.
This medication requires a 36-h washout period when switching from an ACEI to avoid angioedema, but you can start it immediately if switching from an ARB

Known by many names such as 'mediterranean fever' or 'Bang's disease', this disease is notorious for being aerosolized easily, making it one of the most common laboratory-acquired infections in the world

A US eradiation campaign in 1934 cost over $3.5 billion and is one of the most successful livestock programs in history. Now most cases occur in the US after travel although isolated reservoirs of disease remain in wild bison and elk in the Greater Yellowstone Area
Characteristically, patients develop recurring fevers, fatigue, and joint pain after eating raw, unpasteurized milk and cheese from infected animals
